forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KRRE6XMJAK3WH3EVMPTMZX8M
+- 01KRRE7WP7YY56R7MTBHJHZD12
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8d80
+project: plugin-arch
+title: 'plugin: callback primitive for plugin→host function references'
+---
+## What
+Implement the single callback primitive the SDK uses wherever a plugin hands the host a function (command `available`/`execute`, view `render`, event handlers, elicitation responses). Functions can't cross the host/plugin boundary directly.
+
+SDK side (`crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/`) + host side (`crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/`):
+- When the SDK dispatches a call whose input contains function values, it assigns each a callback id, stores `{id → fn}` locally in the isolate, and replaces the function in the outgoing payload with `{ "$callback": "cb_xxxx" }`.
+- The host treats `$callback` markers as opaque handles. To invoke one, the host sends `notifications/callbacks/invoke { id, args }` into the isolate.
+- The SDK receives the notification, looks up the stored function, runs it, and — if a return value is expected — sends `notifications/callbacks/result`.
+- This is the ONE primitive behind event subscriptions, command handlers, view renderers, async streaming, and elicitation.
+- Explicitly NOT used for MCP tool calls themselves — those cross via the transports' real MCP JSON-RPC. The primitive is purely plugin→host function references.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Functions in a dispatched payload are replaced with `{$callback: id}` markers; the `{id→fn}` table lives in the isolate.
+- [ ] `notifications/callbacks/invoke` runs the stored function; a return value flows back via `notifications/callbacks/result`.
+- [ ] Tool-call payloads are unaffected — no `$callback` machinery on the transport path.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test through a real isolate: a plugin passes a function to a host-invoked seam; the test triggers `callbacks/invoke` and asserts the function ran and its return value arrived back where it is awaited (observe via a value the plugin writes or returns).
+- [ ] Test a callback that returns a value vs. one that does not — both behave correctly.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-plugin` — all green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the callback round-trip test first, then implement.
+
+## Depends on
+deno_core runtime + TypeScript SDK.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -34,4 +34,15 @@
 - Use `/tdd` — write the callback round-trip test first, then implement.

 ## Depends on
-deno_core runtime + TypeScript SDK.
\ No newline at end of file
+deno_core runtime + TypeScript SDK.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-17 18:42)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:182` — `marshalCallbacks` recurses unconditionally into plain objects and arrays with no visited-set or depth guard. A callback-bearing payload containing a cyclic plain object (e.g. `const a = {}; a.self = a; callbackDispatch({ a })`) recurses until the V8 stack overflows. This is a legitimately-shaped plugin input, and the function's own JSDoc claims it walks "to any depth" — implying safety it does not provide. The blast is contained to the offending isolate (it is plugin-trusted code), but it is still a crash where the doc implies a clean walk. Add a `WeakSet` of already-visited objects/arrays and skip (or error on) a node already seen, then update the JSDoc to state the cycle behavior.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/host.rs:873` — `collect_callback_ids` has the same unbounded recursion as `marshalCallbacks`: a host-side `callbackDispatch` payload with a JSON cycle is impossible to construct from `serde_json` parsing alone, but a deeply nested payload (legal JSON, no cycle) still recurses one stack frame per level and can overflow the host worker thread's stack. Lower priority than the SDK side since `serde_json::Value` cannot be cyclic, but a depth bound (or an explicit work-stack instead of recursion) would make the host walk robust against a hostile-depth payload from a compromised isolate.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/ledger.rs:31-35` — The `CallbackId` doc comment is now stale: it says "The callback primitive … is built by a later task" and "Nothing in this crate produces a `Callback` handle yet." This task is that later task — `host.rs::callback_dispatch` now produces `Callback` handles. Update the comment to describe `CallbackId` as the live id type the callback primitive uses.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/ledger.rs:70-72` — The `RegistrationHandle::Callback` doc comment likewise still says "The callback primitive is a later task; no handle of this variant is produced yet." It is now produced on every `callbackDispatch`. Update to reflect that the variant is live.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:614-617` — `reservedHandler`'s comment says "The callback/event primitive is wired by a later task." The callback *primitive* is wired by this task; only the event API surface (`on`/`subscribe`/…) remains future work. Narrow the comment to say the *event API* is wired later, so it does not read as stale.
\ No newline at end of file

-### Warnings
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:182` — `marshalCallbacks` recurses unconditionally into plain objects and arrays with no visited-set or depth guard. A callback-bearing payload containing a cyclic plain object (e.g. `const a = {}; a.self = a; callbackDispatch({ a })`) recurses until the V8 stack overflows. This is a legitimately-shaped plugin input, and the function's own JSDoc claims it walks "to any depth" — implying safety it does not provide. The blast is contained to the offending isolate (it is plugin-trusted code), but it is still a crash where the doc implies a clean walk. Add a `WeakSet` of already-visited objects/arrays and skip (or error on) a node already seen, then update the JSDoc to state the cycle behavior.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/host.rs:873` — `collect_callback_ids` has the same unbounded recursion as `marshalCallbacks`: a host-side `callbackDispatch` payload with a JSON cycle is impossible to construct from `serde_json` parsing alone, but a deeply nested payload (legal JSON, no cycle) still recurses one stack frame per level and can overflow the host worker thread's stack. Lower priority than the SDK side since `serde_json::Value` cannot be cyclic, but a depth bound (or an explicit work-stack instead of recursion) would make the host walk robust against a hostile-depth payload from a compromised isolate.
-
-### Nits
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/ledger.rs:31-35` — The `CallbackId` doc comment is now stale: it says "The callback primitive … is built by a later task" and "Nothing in this crate produces a `Callback` handle yet." This task is that later task — `host.rs::callback_dispatch` now produces `Callback` handles. Update the comment to describe `CallbackId` as the live id type the callback primitive uses.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/ledger.rs:70-72` — The `RegistrationHandle::Callback` doc comment likewise still says "The callback primitive is a later task; no handle of this variant is produced yet." It is now produced on every `callbackDispatch`. Update to reflect that the variant is live.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:614-617` — `reservedHandler`'s comment says "The callback/event primitive is wired by a later task." The callback *primitive* is wired by this task; only the event API surface (`on`/`subscribe`/…) remains future work. Narrow the comment to say the *event API* is wired later, so it does not read as stale.

-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/host.rs:873` — `collect_callback_ids` has the same unbounded recursion as `marshalCallbacks`: a host-side `callbackDispatch` payload with a JSON cycle is impossible to construct from `serde_json` parsing alone, but a deeply nested payload (legal JSON, no cycle) still recurses one stack frame per level and can overflow the host worker thread's stack. Lower priority than the SDK side since `serde_json::Value` cannot be cyclic, but a depth bound (or an explicit work-stack instead of recursion) would make the host walk robust against a hostile-depth payload from a compromised isolate.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:182` — `marshalCallbacks` recurses unconditionally into plain objects and arrays with no visited-set or depth guard. A callback-bearing payload containing a cyclic plain object (e.g. `const a = {}; a.self = a; callbackDispatch({ a })`) recurses until the V8 stack overflows. This is a legitimately-shaped plugin input, and the function's own JSDoc claims it walks "to any depth" — implying safety it does not provide. The blast is contained to the offending isolate (it is plugin-trusted code), but it is still a crash where the doc implies a clean walk. Add a `WeakSet` of already-visited objects/arrays and skip (or error on) a node already seen, then update the JSDoc to state the cycle behavior.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/host.rs:873` — `collect_callback_ids` has the same unbounded recursion as `marshalCallbacks`: a host-side `callbackDispatch` payload with a JSON cycle is impossible to construct from `serde_json` parsing alone, but a deeply nested payload (legal JSON, no cycle) still recurses one stack frame per level and can overflow the host worker thread's stack. Lower priority than the SDK side since `serde_json::Value` cannot be cyclic, but a depth bound (or an explicit work-stack instead of recursion) would make the host walk robust against a hostile-depth payload from a compromised isolate.

 ### Nits
-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/ledger.rs:31-35` — The `CallbackId` doc comment is now stale: it says "The callback primitive … is built by a later task" and "Nothing in this crate produces a `Callback` handle yet." This task is that later task — `host.rs::callback_dispatch` now produces `Callback` handles. Update the comment to describe `CallbackId` as the live id type the callback primitive uses.
-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/ledger.rs:70-72` — The `RegistrationHandle::Callback` doc comment likewise still says "The callback primitive is a later task; no handle of this variant is produced yet." It is now produced on every `callbackDispatch`. Update to reflect that the variant is live.
-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/plugin.ts:614-617` — `reservedHandler`'s comment says "The callback/event primitive is wired by a later task." The callback *primitive* is wired by this task; only the event API surface (`on`/`subscribe`/…) remains future work. Narrow the comment to say the *event API* is wired later, so it does not read as stale.
